title: Blood clot in lungs caused Prateek Yadav’s death, reveals post-mortem report
content: In a tragic turn of events, Prateek Yadav, the son of the late Mulayam Singh Yadav, succumbed to a severe blood clot that obstructed his lungs, resulting in heart and respiratory failure. Autopsy reports reveal that he experienced a 'cardiorespiratory collapse due to massive pulmonary thromboembolism.

title: ‘Removed for foreign coach’: Sreejesh slams HI, asks: ‘Can’t Indians develop hockey?’
content: Hockey legend PR Sreejesh expressed shock after his abrupt removal as junior men's coach, despite leading the team to five podium finishes in five tournaments, including a World Cup bronze. He revealed Hockey India's preference for foreign coaches, citing the senior team's chief coach's recommendation, questioning the development of Indian talent.

title: Trump's 'CEO crew' to Beijing is almost as rich as world's third-largest economy
content: Donald Trump has landed in Beijing with 17 American companies in tow,   worth a combined $16.47 trillion—roughly 90% of China's entire 2025 GDP. Jensen Huang joined at the last minute, boarding Air Force One in Anchorage. Nvidia and Apple alone account for $9.7 trillion of that, while Meta sent its policy chief after Beijing blocked its $2 billion Manus deal.

title: Jamie Dimon, CEO of America's largest bank JPMorgan is worried about stock market
content: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon expressed concern over market exuberance, warning investors may be overlooking geopolitical and economic risks like US-Europe trade issues and Middle East tensions. Despite strong trading volumes benefiting Wall Street, Dimon cautioned that broader market optimism might be unjustified.

title: NEET UG paper leak case: CBI arrests five accused, raids multiple locations
content: The CBI has arrested five individuals and launched nationwide searches in connection with alleged irregularities and a paper leak for the NEET UG-2026 examination. The investigation follows a complaint from the Union education ministry, with charges including criminal conspiracy and cheating. The NTA had previously cancelled the exam, affecting nearly 23 lakh candidates, due to suspected circulation of leaked questions.

title: Air India suspends, reduces frequency on 29 int'l routes till Aug - full list
content: Air India is suspending and reducing flights on 29 international routes between June and August 2026 due to the West Asia conflict, rising jet fuel prices, and airspace restrictions. These temporary changes aim to improve operational stability and mitigate passenger inconvenience amid global aviation disruptions. The airline will continue operating over 1,200 international flights monthly across five continents.

title: 'I don’t know how Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will last': AB de Villiers raises alarm
content: AB de Villiers lauded Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's white-ball prowess, but cautioned that his long-term success hinges on adapting to multi-format cricket. While Sooryavanshi's IPL and U19 numbers are exceptional, de Villiers stressed the need for exposure to ODIs and Tests to truly test his mettle and uncover new layers of his game.

title: Microsoft Israel has been placed under the management of Microsoft France, here's why
content: Microsoft Israel's chief has resigned following an internal probe into the misuse of Azure cloud services by the Israeli Ministry of Defense.  The investigation revealed ethical breaches and a lack of transparency, impacting Microsoft's global standing.  This comes after earlier revelations about Unit 8200's surveillance activities, complicating future contract renewals.

title: Mike Vrabel praised by Patriots as Dianna Russini faces career turmoil
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach’s life has remain unchanged as he has resumed his professional duties amid his alleged affair drama with Dianna Russini, sparking chaos among millions. While the outrage against Dianna Russini was so strong that she had to step down from her prestigious role at The Athletic, it seems Mike Vrabel continues to be a favorite one in the team.

title: 'Don't you understand extent of our victory?' Vijay lambasts DMK after floor test win
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay's government won a trust vote with 144 MLAs supporting it. Following the victory, Vijay penned an open letter to the public, criticizing the opposition DMK for attempting to undermine his administration. He highlighted his party's electoral performance and commitment to secular values, while accusing the DMK of hypocrisy in coalition politics.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sold their electrical co for $1.7bn and gave 540 workers $240 m of it
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: CBSE Class 12th result: Why students scored lower this year as pass percentage falls
content: CBSE declared the Class XII results on May 13, 2026, with an overall pass percentage of 85.20%, down from 88.39% last year. Over 15 lakh students cleared the examination out of 17.68 lakh who appeared. The decline of 3.19 percentage points coincided with the introduction of On-Screen Marking (OSM) for evaluation and reportedly tougher Physics and Mathematics papers. Experts cited multiple factors, including digital assessment and competency-based questions under NEP.

title: Mouni Roy, Suraj Nambiar split six months ago, divorce yet to be filed: Report
content: Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar's marriage is reportedly over, with sources revealing they separated six months ago. While divorce hasn't been filed, Mouni has consulted lawyers.  Speculation arose after they unfollowed each other on Instagram and Mouni removed couple photos.  Reports suggest Suraj felt overshadowed by Mouni's fame.

title: Cruise ship with 1,700 passengers onboard isolated after suspected norovirus death
content: Over 1,700 people are confined to a cruise ship in Bordeaux after a 90-year-old passenger died from a suspected norovirus infection. Approximately 50 others onboard are exhibiting symptoms of the highly contagious stomach bug. Health officials are implementing strict measures to prevent further spread of the virus.

title: Virat Kohli’s strong record against KKR faces toughest challenge in Sunil Narine
content: Virat Kohli, IPL's leading run-scorer, faces Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026. Despite a strong season, he seeks to break a rare duck streak. Kohli boasts an impressive record against KKR, but faces a significant challenge from Sunil Narine, who has historically contained him.

title: 'EPS has no such idea': Rebel AIADMK MLA lobs 'betrayer' charge at party chief
content: AIADMK faces a severe internal crisis as a faction of MLAs, led by C.V. Shanmugam, openly rebelled against party chief Edappadi Palaniswami. They allege EPS explored aligning with the DMK and abandoned the party's core anti-DMK stance.

title: Hardik Pandya Update: After over 3 hrs training, will he play PBKS match?
content: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya's intense training session fuels speculation amid his absence due to back spasms. While the team cites recovery, his vigorous workout raises questions about his availability. Pandya and stand-in skipper Suryakumar Yadav are currently away from the squad, impacting the team's forgettable season where they have already been eliminated from playoff race.

title: The US has lost the reputation war — to Russia and China
content: A new global survey reveals a significant drop in America's international image under President Trump, now trailing behind Russia and China.  Concerns over his foreign policy, tariffs, and military actions have unsettled allies and created global unease.  This decline marks the second consecutive year of falling perceptions for the US.

title: Assam cabinet clears draft UCC Bill, to be tabled in Assembly on May 26
content: Assam's cabinet has approved the Uniform Civil Code Bill, slated for introduction in the Assembly on May 26. This move aligns with the BJP's commitment to a common set of civil laws for marriage, divorce, and inheritance, aiming for legal uniformity across communities. The state government emphasises prioritising Assam's identity and development agenda while enacting this significant legislation.

title: Mike Vrabel’s marriage rocked after Dianna Russini clip resurfaces online
content: A resurfaced 2020 video featuring Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini has reignited online speculation surrounding their alleged relationship. The clip shows Russini giving intimacy advice during a Barstool segment while Vrabel jokingly responds, “I’m paying very close attention… I’m so excited.” The renewed attention comes after recent photos and videos of the pair went viral. Although no divorce or affair has been officially confirmed, the controversy continues fueling intense discussion across NFL and social media circles.

title: ISL clubs propose club-led model for operations, commercialisation
content: Indian Super League clubs, excluding East Bengal, have proposed a club-led model for future league operations and commercialisation. They aim to manage responsibilities, citing local market knowledge and cost-saving potential. This proposal comes ahead of a crucial meeting with the AIFF president Kalyan Chaubey on May 22.

title: Trains at 220 kmph on Indian Railways soon! Cabinet approves project; check details
content: The proposed rail line is expected to improve connectivity between Ahmedabad, Dholera Special Investment Region, the upcoming Dholera International Airport and the Lothal National Maritime Heritage Complex.

title: The Siraj-Rabada powerplay blueprint: Test-match lengths, T20 destruction
content: Mohammad Siraj and Kagiso Rabada have become IPL 2026's most lethal new-ball pair, bowling the entire powerplay in five consecutive games. Their relentless 'Test-match length' bowling, consistently hitting good and back-of-length areas, ripped through SRH's top order, claiming 4 wickets within the first 6 overs. This bowling prowess has been the driving force behind Gujarat Titans' success this season.

title: 'Not a big deal': Rajasthan minister Madan Dilawar downplays NEET-UG 2026 exam cancellation
content: Rajasthan's Education Minister downplayed the NEET-UG 2026 cancellation, calling it "not a big issue" if irregularities occurred. However, Congress leaders accused the state government of suppressing information about the alleged paper leak, which involved a multi-state network and a "guess paper" sold for lakhs.

title: Rupee at 95.74, hits all-time low as oil prices pressure economy
content: The Indian rupee hit a new low against the US dollar, pressured by rising oil prices and external debt. Analysts link the decline to the US-Iran conflict, which has boosted crude oil costs and strained India's economy. While the RBI and government are intervening, markets anticipate potential interest rate hikes to stabilize the currency and manage inflation.

title: Gunfire outside Diljit Dosanjh manager’s residence: Lawrence Bishnoi gang claims responsibility
content: Unidentified assailants opened fire outside the residence of Diljit Dosanjh’s manager in Haryana, with the Lawrence Bishnoi gang claiming responsibility. The group alleges the attack was a warning over alleged mistreatment of women by a member of Dosanjh's team during an Australian tour.

title: Gordon Ramsay's son-in-law Adam Peaty to race as Adam Ramsay-Peaty at Commonwealth Games
content: Three-time Olympic champion Adam Peaty is preparing for a historic moment at the upcoming Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. The British swimmer will compete under his new name, Adam Ramsay-Peaty, following his marriage to Holly Ramsay late last year. The 31-year-old remains the senior figure in Team England’s 42-member swimming squad and is aiming to become the first British swimmer to win gold medals at four consecutive Commonwealth Games.

title: Prabhsimran Singh shuts down 'fake narratives' with 'shushing' emoji Insta story
content: Punjab Kings opener Prabhsimran Singh has responded to online criticism regarding his form and fitness, echoing co-owner Preity Zinta's stance against misinformation. Despite recent struggles, Singh remains a key scorer for PBKS, who are facing a crucial must-win match against Mumbai Indians after a four-game losing streak.

title: Kuki church leaders ambushed in Manipur: 3 clergy members killed en route to peace meeting
content: Three Kuki church leaders were killed and four injured in an armed ambush in Manipur's Kangpokpi district while traveling to an inter-association ecclesiastical meeting. Community sources and the Kuki Organisation for Human Rights Trust alleged involvement of NSCN-IM and ZUF, calling it a premeditated attack targeting peace efforts.

title: Months after divorce posts, Prateek and Aparna Yadav shared pics together of vacation in Iceland
content: Weeks after a public marital dispute, Prateek Yadav and BJP leader Aparna Yadav vacationed in Iceland with their daughter, sharing photos of a seemingly resolved conflict. The trip followed Prateek's accusations of Aparna prioritizing fame and his subsequent announcement of reconciliation, just months before his sudden death.

title: ED tightens grip on Nashik astrologer ‘Bhondu Baba’, summons NCP leader Rupali Chakankar
content: The ED has directed Chakankar to appear before the agency for questioning later this week as part of its ongoing probe into the matter. Previously, the ED had also questioned Chakankar's sister in connection with the same case.

title: 'Big plus for RCB': Mohammad Kaif highlights major Varun Chakaravarthy concern for KKR
content: Former India batter Mohammad Kaif believes spinner Varun Chakaravarthy's potential absence against RCB would be a significant boost for Bengaluru. Chakaravarthy's recent return to form has been crucial for KKR's winning streak, but an injury concern looms. Kaif highlighted Virat Kohli's comfort against faster spinners, suggesting his absence would benefit RCB.

title: Russia tests ‘world’s most powerful missile’: How RS-28 Sarmat ‘Satan II’ works
content: Russia has successfully tested its new RS-28 Sarmat intercontinental ballistic missile, dubbed 'Satan II' by NATO. President Putin hailed it as the world's most powerful nuclear weapon, capable of carrying multiple warheads and evading missile defenses.

title: Bengal CM Suvendu retains Bhabanipur, seat where he defeated Mamata, over Nandigram
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has chosen to keep the Bhabanipur seat and will vacate Nandigram, despite winning both in the recent assembly elections. Adhikari, who previously defeated Mamata Banerjee in Nandigram, had challenged her again by contesting from Bhabanipur. He stated his inability to hold MLA positions from two constituencies and has taken his oath from Bhabanipur.

title: Vijay clears floor test with 144 MLAs backing TVK: How the high-stakes assembly showdown unfolded
content: Tamil Nadu CM Joseph Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) successfully passed a crucial floor test, securing the support of several parties including a rebel AIADMK faction. This marks the first coalition government led by a new entrant, breaking the decades-long Dravidian political dominance. Vijay's victory solidifies his position as the state's first non-DMK, non-AIADMK chief minister since 1967.

title: NIA arrests narco-terror accused Iqbal Singh alias Shera after extradition from Portugal
content: National Investigation Agency has arrested Iqbal Singh alias Shera. He is a wanted narco-terrorist. His extradition from Portugal followed diplomatic and legal efforts. Shera is considered the mastermind in a Hizb-ul-Mujahideen terror financing case. His arrest is a significant breakthrough in tackling Pakistan-backed narco-terror networks.

title: ‘Unfair trade practice’: EPFO pulled up over PF delay, awards Rs 50,000 compensation
content: Chandigarh Consumer Commission penalised EPFO Rs 50,000 for a decade-long delay in transferring provident fund. Software glitches were dismissed as an excuse for the significant lapse. The commission found EPFO guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice, ordering compensation for the employee's prolonged wait for his PF funds.

title: GT’s 2nd-half IPL surge explained: Holder gamble, bowling brutality, and?
content: Gujarat Titans, after a slow start in IPL 2026, are on the cusp of playoff qualification with eight wins from twelve matches. Their resurgence is fueled by a dominant opening pair, the impactful inclusion of Jason Holder, and a lethal bowling attack that has stifled opposition batting.

title: School education index: Kerala betters grade, score
content: Kerala has received the grade Prachesta-2 and scored 687.7 in the Union Ministry of Education’s Performance Grading Index 2.0 for the year 2024-25

title: Assam filmmaker’s documentary screened at British Film Institute in London
content: Chalo India with Ericji (Varanasi) was showcased at the BFI after its selection in the Indian Panorama Non-Feature section at the 56th International Film Festival of India

title: Bihar Cabinet hikes DA, clears 19 major development proposals
content: Declares EV incentives; approves transfer of 110.12 acres to CISF to set up recruitment training Centre in Kishanganj

title: Women in Cinema Collective joins fundraising drive for legal fight by survivor in Kerala actor rape case
content: Aim of Avalkoppam Legal Support Trust is to provide support to survivors in cases of abuse against women

title: Security stepped up for visit of Chandrababu Naidu, Rajnath Singh to Puttaparthi on May 15
content: The leaders are scheduled to lay foundation stone for the ₹1 lakh-crore defence manufacturing facility that is expected to emerge as a major aerospace hub in the region and generate large-scale employment opportunities

title: Pawan Khera appears before Assam police Crime Branch
content: The Congress leader is being questioned in connection with a case filed against him by Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, wife of Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma.

title: NEET paper leak case: CBI arrests five accused; conducts searches across country
content: The Central government has asked the CBI to carry out a comprehensive inquiry into the “irregularities”

title: Beef-eating charge: Poll candidate’s mother to sue Assam CM Himanta for ₹1, seeks apology
content: Sujata Gurung Chowdhury, mother of AJP candidate Kunki Chowdhury, says Himanta Biswa Sarma branded her a ‘beef eater’, ‘non-Sanatani’, and ‘anti-India communist’ without knowing anything about her life, food habits, culture, religion, ideology, or ‘perhaps me as a person’

title: CBI secures extradition of fugitive from Azerbaijan
content: ‘After the subject was geo-located and arrested by the Azerbaijan authorities, an extradition request was sent to them,’ the agency says

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly: Edappadi Palaniswami claims AIADMK rebel MLAs promised Cabinet berths for support to TVK govt.
content: AIADMK MLAs backing Mr. Palaniswami strongly objected to the Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar calling S.P. Velumani, who heads a rebel group in the AIADMK, to speak on the motion.

title: Visakhapatnam police bust ₹400 crore online betting racket; 7 held
content: The Police Commissioner said the kingpin Karan Pakhrani and six associates were arrested from Regent Park in Kolkata; the racket allegedly used 224 mule bank accounts to launder money

title: SC says Harish Rana’s family ‘selfless’ decision to donate organs will keep his legacy alive
content: The top court had held that withdrawal of life-sustaining support in a palliative care setting would be in the best interests of the 31-year-old man, who had remained in a permanent vegetative state for over a decade

title: NEET paper leak case: One detained is Rajasthan BJP leader, claims Ashok Gehlot
content: BJP State Vice-President Mukesh Dadhich said Dinesh Binwal possesses no post in the party while there was no confirmation of arrest from agencies

title: Bengaluru records 93.19 pass percentage in CBSE class 12 examinations
content: According to CBSE data, a total of 22,459 students from the Bengaluru region had registered for the class 12 examinations this year.

title: Vijay’s government revokes order appointing astrologer Rickey Radhan Pandit as OSD (Political) to CM
content: The government, on May 12, appointed him to the post and it drew severe criticism, even from TVK allies

title: Tamil Nadu government appoints Rickey Pandit Vettrivel as OSD (Political) to CM Vijay
content: CPI(M) state secretary P. Shanmugam said that it was unacceptable to appoint an astrologer as the Officer-on-Duty (Politics) to the Chief Minister

title: TVK’s Tirupattur MLA, who won by one vote, not to take part in trust vote in Assembly: Madras High Court
content: Justices L. Victoria Gowri and N. Senthilkumar pass the interim order on a writ petition filed by DMK former Minister K.R. Periakaruppan

title: Cyber Crime Wing notice for blocking social media posts draws criticism
content: Incidentally, most of the flagged URLs/posts allegedly contained content critical of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam and Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay

title: CBSE Class 12 results: Tamil Nadu registers 95.25% pass; girls outperform boys
content: As many as 46,901 boys and 41,245 girls from Tamil Nadu wrote the CBSE Class 12 exams, wherein girls outperformed boys (94%), with a pass rate of 96.68%

title: Prakasam Collector P. Raja Babu inspects OMC sanitation, directs use of vacant lands at Oota Cheruvu
content: The collector visited the windrow composting unit, Gandhi Park and the ongoing Sulabh Complex construction during the surprise inspection

title: India should have stepped into vacuum left by U.S. as guarantor of security in West Asia: Talmiz Ahmad
content: Speaking on the topic ‘India’s Diplomatic Balancing in West Asia: Strategic Autonomy, Energy Security and Global Responsibility’, the former Ambassador remarked that “India is not a serious role-player as far as West Asia and the crisis over the last two and a half years is concerned.”

title: Thangal to respond after Kerala CM announcement
content: Leaders reportedly expressed that Congress’s indecision had dented the UDF’s public image and weakened the alliance’s momentum following its sweeping electoral victory

title: ‘Savukku’ Shankar gets bail in attempt-to-murder case
content: A habeas corpus petition has also been filed in the Madras High Court challenging his preventive detention under the Goondas Act

title: Iran war shows military force alone cannot be solution to conflicts: T.S. Tirumurti
content: The former Ambassador and Permanent Representative of India to the United Nations was speaking at the inauguration of Diplomacy and Sustainability Dialogues 2026, jointly organised by Saveetha Institute of Medical and Technical Sciences (SIMATS) and The Hindu

title: Cabinet clears upgradation of Nagpur International Airport
content: With the extension of the lease period, the Civil Aviation Ministry said it would now become co-terminus with the 30-year concession period of GMR Nagpur International Airport Ltd

title: N. Rangasamy sworn in as Puducherry Chief Minister for 5th time
content: Along with Mr. Rangasamy, Bharatiya Janata Party’s legislature party leader A. Namassivayam and AINRC legislator Malladi Krishna Rao were sworn in as Ministers in the second NDA government in the UT

title: Air India cuts international flights; suspends services on 6 overseas routes
content: Air India said the latest announced rationalisation of services will be in place during the June-August period

title: SC asks Centre to consider States’ pleas for funds for CCTVs in police stations
content: The top court had initiated a suo motu proceeding over non-functional CCTV cameras in police stations linked to a spike in custodial deaths in recent months

title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled: J&K Congress protests in Jammu, demands Supreme Court-monitored probe
content: Protesters raised slogans against the BJP govt and burnt an effigy of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, seeking a Supreme Court-monitored probe into the alleged irregularities linked to the NEET examination

title: NEET-UG 2026: Investigation finds MBBS student in Kerala forwarded guess paper to friends in Rajasthan
content: An official from the SOG said a student from Kerala forwarded the guess paper to a some of his friends abd a hostel owner in Sikar

title: KCBC calls for comprehensive rehaul of Kerala’s liquor policy towards total prohibition in five years
content: Call for rewriting liquor policy along the lines of the model implemented by new Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay

title: Deepak Joy resigns as deputy mayor and councillor of Kochi corporation
content: Both vacancies were immediately reported to the State Election Commission, as per norm; a by-election to fill the vacancy must be held within six months

title: Sanatana Dharma row: TTD board member Bhanu Prakash Reddy criticises Udhayanidhi Stalin
content: G. Bhanu Prakash Reddy faulted Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay for remaining silent over Udhayanidhi’s remarks in the Assembly

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly session updates: Chief Minister Vijay's TVK wins trust vote with 144 ‘ayes’
content: Udhayanidhi led DMK MLAs' walkout prior to voting; 25 rebel AIADMK MLAs from C.Ve. Shanmugam camp extended support to TVK while 22 AIADMK MLAs of the Edappadi Palaniswami faction voted against the confidence motion.

title: Telangana CM suggests Uber to establish parking centres and EV charging stations; firm reveals Hyderabad CoE expansion plans
content: Uber CEO evinces interest in setting up the firm’s corporate office in the upcoming Bharat Future City

title: NIA arrests Hizb-ul-Mujahideen linked ‘narco-terrorist’ after extradition from Portugal
content: According to the NIA, Shera was a key conspirator and handler of an India-based narco-terror module engaged in smuggling heroin from Pakistan

title: The ‘Kutty Story’ phenomenon: How Vijay prepared voters with his audio launch speeches
content: From ‘Kutty Stories’ that captivated the youth to punchlines that advised them to aim big and live better, Vijay’s captivating speeches have been instrumental in transforming his film fandom into a robust voter base

title: George Kutty, a beloved figure in Bengaluru’s film society movement, no more
content: As the driving force behind the Bangalore Film Society and the editor of Deep Focus magazine, George Kutty brought thought-provoking and socially relevant films to the forefront

title: ‘Athiradi’ trailer: Tovino Thomas and Basil Joseph lock horns in this action-comedy film
content: The film, set to release on May 14, is directed by debutant Arun Anirudhan, who penned ‘Minnal Murali’ and ‘Padayottam’

title: OPT fraud: 10,000 foreign students, including Indians, under U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement scanner
content: “Our nation will not tolerate security threats originating from the foreign student programme,” says ICE’s acting director Todd Lyons

title: Congress unveils ‘Bhu Guarantee’ in Karnataka
content: The announcements include a reduction in the guidance value cost that needs to be paid for conversion from B khata to A khata, and regularisation of buildings in BDA areas constructed before 2008

title: NEET-UG cancellation: ABVP workers protest outside NTA office in Delhi
content: Heavy security arrangements were put in place outside the NTA office to prevent any untoward incident

title: BJP strips elderly, poor of economic dignity while spending on political spectacle: Congress
content: Between 2014-15 and 2024-25, the BJP Government spent a total of Rs 5,987.46 Cr on self-promotion in the form of advertisements, says Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge

title: Kerala HC seeks report on siblings approaching Collector to end parents’ feud
content: The children had earlier approached the child welfare committee, citing that they were emotionally exhausted due to the feud between their parents and police cases

title: Foundation stone laid for ₹700-crore BISER medical college and 500-bed hospital in Amaravati
content: APCRDA has allotted 23 acres of land on lease at Nidamarru in Mangalagiri mandal for the project backed by KIMS

title: Pinarayi Vijayan calls for scrapping NTA, demands time-bound probe into NEET leak allegations
content: He called for handing back the responsibility for conducting examinations to the respective State government agencies as was done earlier

title: Sonia Gandhi admitted to Gurugram hospital for minor medical procedure
content: The hospital authorities have not released any details about the leader’s health status or the treatment/ procedure she is to undergo.

title: Trainer aircraft of private company crash lands in Baramati; no casualty reported
content: “The aircraft was flying at a comparatively low altitude when it developed a technical malfunction, and one one side of the aircraft reportedly hit a light pole before it struck the ground,” Superintendent of Police, Pune, said

title: Video: Shoulder Brush At Karnataka Gym Ends In Punch, Skull Fracture
content: A video of the incident showed Keerthan brushing past Varun's shoulder, following which the two briefly confronted each other inside the gym.

title: Assam Cabinet Approves Uniform Civil Code In First Post-Election Meet
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma said that the proposed UCC in Assam has been "customised" to protect tribal communities and preserve religious customs and traditions.

title: Small Convoys, Travel Curbs: BJP Leaders' Initiatives After PM Modi's Fuel Appeal
content: From Uttar Pradesh to Gujarat, strict directives have been issued to curtail government expenditure and reduce fuel consumption.

title: Wayanad To Be Next Amethi: Posters Warn Gandhis On Kerala Chief Minister Pick
content: Posters have come up in Kerala's Wayanad -- the constituency of Priyanka Gandhi Vadra -- warning Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi against picking KC Venugopal for the Kerala Chief Minister post.

title: Trump China Visit Live Updates: Trump In China For Talks With Xi On Iran War, US Arms Sales To Taiwan
content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: Hours After TVK's Floor Test Win, Vijay Calls DMK 'Arrogant'
content: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: A rebel faction of the AIADMK, comprising 30 MLAs, said they will support Vijay in Wednesday's floor test.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Ceasefire At Risk As Iran Rejects US Plan, Trump Calls Response 'Garbage'
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: PM Modi's Appeal Not About Spending Less But Spending Wisely: Sources
content: Government sources have pushed back against descriptions of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent appeal for energy conservation and reduced foreign exchange outflows as "austerity measures", saying the term is misleading and carries negative economi

title: Uber Partners With Adani Group To Set Up First Data Centre In India
content: Ahmedabad, May 13 (IANS) Ride-hailing major Uber is setting up its first data centre in India in partnership with the Adani Group as the country rapidly emerges as a major innovation hub for the company, Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi said on Wednesday.

title: Gujarat Police Bans Public Shaming, Parading Of Accused In New Directive
content: The new guidelines mandate that police officers must ensure the self-respect and dignity of an accused person remains intact from the moment of arrest through every stage of the investigation.

title: France Confines 1,700 On Cruise Ship After Suspected Norovirus Death
content: French authorities on Wednesday confined more than 1,700 passengers and crew on a cruise ship docked in Bordeaux after a passenger died from a suspected norovirus, officials said.

title: Live Updates: Kerala Chief Minister Announcement Likely Today After Big Congress Meet
content: Kerala Chief Minister Live Updates: A formal announcement is expected today, once Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge returns to Delhi and holds further talks with senior figures, including Sonia Gandhi.

title: "Baseless": Nepal On Reports Of New Restrictions On Indian Tourists
content: Nepal stressed that the country remains a safe, welcoming, and friendly destination for visitors from India and around the world.

title: 'Tumhari Behen Zinda Nahi Aayegi ': Bihar Woman Dies 4 Days After Wedding
content: During the four days that Sanju and Vinod's marriage lasted before the bride was killed, she was allegedly physically and mentally assaulted.

title: Man Arrested For Opening Fire At Delhi Wedding That Injured Woman
content: According to police, the incident took place on the night of April 11 in northeast Delhi when a wedding procession was passing through the Dayalpur area.

title: New Labour Code Opens A Door To 4-Day Work Week, But Conditions Apply
content: Code on Wages (Central) Rules: Under the new provision, the condensed work week typically could mean 12-hour workdays for four days.

title: Renewable Energy Capacity Up 20%: How Much Will India Save On Fuel Imports?
content: India Green Energy: Amid PM Modi's push for fuel conservation, India's renewable generation capacity has risen 20% year-on-year.

title: Suvendu Adhikari Keeps Bhabanipur Seat, Vacates Nandigram After Winning Both
content: He defeated TMC supremo Mamata Banerjee in her Bhabanipur bastion in the recently concluded Assembly polls by a margin of 15,105

title: Meet Ashok Bahar: 71-year-old UP man whose dream of clearing NEET was shattered by paper leak

title: The Dragon & the Dealmaker: Tariff warrior Trump arrives in China as trade pilgrim
content: the visit comes wrapped in imperial pomp and choreographed grandeur . under the ceremony lies a more transactional reality: Trump is effectively in China hunting for a trade deal that can ease economic anxieties at home . the symbolism of the trip is striking .
